mechanism_narrative: Persistent high federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average (t-test p>0.05), or if Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window is less than 0.03 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate below 4% signals an extremely tight labor market that compresses equity risk premiums and supports profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses below 4.0, the subsequent 20-day forward return median is not significantly above zero (one-sample sign test p>0.05), or if the signal produces more than 50% negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate robust demand and supply conditions, fueling corporate earnings and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: If when both series are above their respective 3-month moving averages, the 60-day forward return median is not positive (Wilcoxon test p>0.05), or if the joint signal does not outperform individual signals in a regression.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of low inflation (CPI year-over-year below 2%), a rise in consumer prices signals healthy demand growth that supports earnings and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: If during periods with CPI y/y < 2%, a positive monthly change in CPIAUCSL is not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the strategy produces negative mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (-10% or more) signals incipient weakness in the housing sector, a leading indicator for the economy, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If after a >10% monthly drop in HOUST, the 5-day forward return is not negative with at least 60% hit rate, or if average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is growing strongly (above 150k per month) and the fed funds rate is rising, the economy is in late-cycle overheating, and markets anticipate tightening and recession risk, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: If when PAYEMS growth is above 150k/month and FEDFUNDS is above its 6-month lagged average, the 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative with a t-test p<0.05, or if the combination does not improve over individual signals.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which is positively correlated with corporate profits and equity market returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: If the Spearman correlation between INDPRO level (3-month lagged) and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive at the 95% level over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a consumer recession (retail sales year-over-year negative), further declines in retail sales signal deepening economic contraction and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If during periods with RSAFS y/y < 0%, a negative monthly change in RSAFS is not followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a hit rate above 55%.
